label: Localized common wart without genital or immunocompromised-host red flags
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized common wart.
No genital wart/STI pathway, near-eye lesion, infection, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or diagnostic uncertainty.
exclusion_criteria: Genital wart/STI pathway, near-eye lesion, infection concern, immunocompromised host, rapidly enlarging/bleeding lesion, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, dermatology, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports wart framing, spread prevention, and care escalation for painful, infected, or unusual lesions. Autonomous expansion approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed localized common wart without genital/STI pathway, infection, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or diagnostic uncertainty.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports wart framing, spread prevention, and care escalation for painful, infected, or unusual lesions. Autonomous expansion approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed localized common wart without genital/STI pathway, infection, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or diagnostic uncertainty.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized common wart
localized skin wart with no infection, immune-risk context, diagnostic uncertainty, or specialist-directed plan documented
